_meta.run: LongMemEval-M full-haystack, full engine (spine-sort + per-topic consolidation ledgers + dream-tier retrieval) — multi-session category
_meta.scope: THIS LEDGER COVERS ONLY THE MULTI-SESSION CATEGORY (8 of the 48 questions). The other 40 questions were never re-run with the engine — see composition_note.
_meta.composition_note: The 72.9% (35/48) headline is COMPOSED, not measured in a single 48-question run: 30/40 carried unchanged from the baseline ledger (5 other categories, never retried with the engine) + 5/8 from this ledger. Run `node verify.js` to see the composition recomputed. This is why 72.9% is reported as a lower bound.
_meta.replay_rule: Per METHODOLOGY.md §4.1, a HIT counts only if it reproduces on a second independent run of the same config. Both runs are named per row in `runs`. Two questions produced a HIT on their first run and did NOT reproduce; they are scored MISS here and the discarded verdict is kept visible rather than deleted.
_meta.note: generated/answer fields are truncated excerpts for readability; full transcripts available on request. verdict = final llm_judge decision.

generated: Based on the memory context, you need to pick up or return a total of 3 items of clothing.

runs.answer: Based on the provided memory, you have a total of 3 clothing items to either pick up from or return to a store.
Based on the memory context, you need to pick up or return a total of 3 items of clothing.

generated: Based on the memory context, you have led or are currently leading a total of 10 projects.
miss_class: benchmark-structural: other personas' first-person statements in the shared haystack are indistinguishable from the user's, causing over-count (see METHODOLOGY.md §5)

runs.answer: Based on the memory context, you have led or are currently leading a total of 9 projects.
Based on the memory context, you have led or are currently leading a total of 10 projects.

expected: I have worked on or bought five model kits.
generated: Based on the provided memory context, you have worked on or bought a total of 6 model kits.
discard_reason: judge noise (false positive). The engine gave the SAME answer — 6 kits — in both runs; the ground truth is five. The first run's judge scored it HIT, the replay scored it MISS. Scored MISS here per the replay rule.
miss_class: benchmark-structural: distractor-persona contamination inflates the count (see METHODOLOGY.md §5)

runs.answer: Based on the memory context, you have worked on or bought a total of 6 model kits.
Based on the provided memory context, you have worked on or bought a total of 6 model kits.

expected: 15 hours for getting to the three destinations (or 30 hours for the round trip)
generated: Based on the memory context, the total driving time for two of your road trips is 11 hours.
discard_reason: answer variance (not judge noise). The first run answered 15 hours and was correctly scored HIT; the replay answered 11 hours — it found only two of the three trips — and was correctly scored MISS. The verdict did not reproduce, so it is scored MISS here per the replay rule.
miss_class: write/read coverage trade-off: the third trip's evidence sits one position past a 40-chunk-per-topic write cap (see METHODOLOGY.md §5 and the campaign summary, root cause #3)

runs.answer: Based on the memory context, driving times are specified for two road trip destinations: Washington D.C. 6 hours, Mountains in Tennessee... total 15 hours.
Based on the memory context, the total driving time for two of your road trips is 11 hours.
